why: The admission request primary key is 32 LOWERCASE hex digits, no dashes (REQUEST_ID_RE cortex validate.ts). Baseline accept.
The dashed 8-4-4-4-12 UUID form is REJECTED (validate.ts). A path param carrying the human UUID form must 400, never be re-derived.
Case-sensitivity: the grammar is lowercase-hex only. An uppercase digit must be rejected so two casings of one id cannot address two rows.
Length is fixed at 32. A short value must be rejected before any body parse or crypto (the M2 fail-fast gate).
requested_scope is built as federated.${principal}.> (principals.ts). Baseline round-trip of the reserved federated prefix + principal segment + '>' tail.

The scope delimiter is '.', not '-', so a hyphen-bearing principal round-trips unambiguously here — unlike the did:mf:{principal}-{stack} join whose '-' separator collides with in-principal hyphens (RFC-0001). Proves the scope decode is NOT vulnerable to the hyphen-separator defect.
The '.>' tail is REQUIRED — the scope is a subtree grant. A prefix without the wildcard is a different (narrower) subject and must not be silently widened.
ADMITTED is the roster-membership source of truth (ADR-0018 Q3). One of the five reserved enum tokens (types.ts).
DEPARTED (member left) and REVOKED (hub kicked) are DISTINCT terminal states the roster must keep separable (admission-read.ts). Collapsing them loses the left-vs-kicked audit distinction.
Case-sensitivity: the enum is UPPERCASE. A case-insensitive parser would accept 'admitted' and silently confer membership — the grammar pins case so ABNF cannot mask it.
GRANTED is the pre-ADR-0015 issuance vocabulary; it MUST NOT be honoured on the admission enum. An unknown token is a fault, never coerced to the nearest state.

Both parties MUST agree byte-for-byte on the signed bytes (canonical-json.ts: sort-keys, no whitespace, undefined skipped). Pins the canonical-JSON profile the entire admission gate signs. NOTE the bytes bind ONLY request_id + decision (+ admin_pubkey/issued_at/nonce): they DO NOT bind peer_pubkey / principal_id / requested_scope / network_id — the admin signature does not commit to WHICH identity is admitted (Security Considerations, decision-claim binding scope).

The hub-admin seal-delivery write (SealedSecretWriteClaim) binds request_id + the opaque sealed ciphertext under the HUB-admin authority (second party, ADR-0018 Q5). Pins its canonical bytes; the claim does NOT bind the ciphertext to the row's peer_pubkey — the hub-admin is trusted to seal to the right key.
v1 is the sovereign-model transport PSK envelope (ADR-0018). Decoders MUST accept it. The sealed plaintext is UTF-8 JSON, version-pinned by v.

The ADR-0019 payload key K rides the SAME sealed slot as an OPTIONAL rider. Decoders MUST tolerate its presence AND absence (sealed-leaf-secret.ts readPayloadKeyFields) — one extra sealed blob, no schema change.

v2 is the operator-mode scoped-user .creds envelope that SUPERSEDED the v1 PSK (ADR-0023, epic #1595). The payload variant is DISCRIMINATED by v (not leaf_psk-XOR-creds) so a version-blind decoder cannot be tricked into a silent payload downgrade (design R9/R12).

A KNOWN-newer version fails closed with a distinct error naming the real remedy (upgrade cortex), NOT the misleading missing-leaf_psk path. A decoder MUST NOT guess a higher-versioned payload (sealed-leaf-secret.ts UnsupportedEnvelopeVersionError).
Fails closed on a malformed v1 envelope — else the member renders a leaf with a junk secret. The decoder never echoes the plaintext in the error.
A string v:"2" must be rejected, not silently mis-routed to the v1 missing-leaf_psk path — the numeric-version guard covers every decoder (parseEnvelopeObject).

MASKING CASE. For every IN-grammar principal the defensive coercion keySegment()/clean() is the identity, so it produces the right key and hides that the function COERCES rather than VALIDATES. This vector passes on both the correct and the buggy implementation — it exists to make the collision pair below meaningful.

COLLISION PAIR. A '.' is not in principal-id's alphabet (RFC-0001) and would open a KV key hierarchy, so an out-of-grammar principal MUST be REJECTED. The deployed keySegment()/clean() instead maps [^a-zA-Z0-9_-] to '-' (gate.ts, provision.ts) yielding 'a-b' — byte-identical to the legitimate principal 'a-b', so two distinct requesters SHARE one rate-limit KV key. The current impl FAILS this vector; that failure IS the finding (Security Considerations, charset-coercion collisions).

'_' is absent from BOTH principal-id (RFC-0001) and the key charset [a-z0-9-], yet keySegment() preserves it (it is in the coercion allowlist [a-zA-Z0-9_-]). A key with an underscore violates the interop key grammar; the segment MUST be rejected upstream.
